name: michael tarvin
body: Prayers still reaching out on your behalf. Keep walking in his strength and peace, as only through Jesus . Bumps in the road may come, but He will hold you steady. ❤️🙏🏼 amy

name: Larry Hickman
body: God has given our extended family so many struggles that have in reality become huge steps of faith building in our journey. As my girls father, the ultimate goal has been that each would discover their own personal love and faith in our God. But I have been so blessed- - not only have they developed this faith but their husbands have shown great leadership in reinforcing God’s goodness to their Families. God is so good all the time and has shown His love to all of us. I am so proud of Lee’s spiritual journey and his parenting. Lyla demonstrates spiritual maturity that her dad and mom have taught and this grandpa can learn from and be blessed by. I constantly find myself smiling when I hear and see Lyla’s reaction to diversity. If we could all be so in tune with God’s larger plan. Job trusted that God was in control. We now are waiting for celebrating the spiritual victories that come from Lyla’s walk in Christ. Your grandpa is so happy with who our Lyla is becoming as a young woman. .

name: Renee Runyon
body: I am so sorry for this enormous disappointment for you all. To know that the possibility of a seizure still exists is very hard, indeed. I feel sad hearing this news, knowing that this part of the journey continues, but I also see the reverse statistic so clearly, however--she had SO many days without even one seizure! God has brought amazing healing to her life, and for that I give Him thanks! May God give you grace for this continuing journey and an extra measure of hope for Lyla's bright future.

name: Julie Hickman
body: I think of one word … endurance. This is not a sprint but more of a marathon. I hate there has been what seems like a setback but we know that ultimately whatever trials we face in this world , Christ has won the victory and that is what gives us the HOPE to sustain us. Her character (and yours) continues to strengthen through every stage of this journey, as exemplify by her praying for her family! What a priceless story. God obviously has big plans for this little girl. Uncle Paul and Aunt Julie

name: Mindy Leach
body: Oh, my heart hurts right now. 😭💔 I honestly have no words. But I know that even without the words to pray, Jesus is interceding at the throne on my behalf for Lyla and your family. Don't give up hope! Love you all so much and I HATE that you are going through this.

name: Nancy Rapaszky
body: Prayers continue. Had you started to back off her meds? I believe that was your plan. Lyla and all of you have such faith. I am so touched by your perspectives. I feel very privileged to know you. May God bless the doctors with wisdom to turn this challenge around.

name: Kari Tarvin
body: Lee, I'm still awake and just praying for you all. Boy did I need to read this post laying here awake at midnight. Thanks for the sweet reminder that no matter the outcome or situation...our God is still good, in control, and very much a part of this journey and Lyla's story. He's not done with her yet. There's so much more to her story. I love you, Lee. I love the incredible dad and husband that you are.
